label: Mark 1:25
verseText: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
jesusText: Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

label: Mark 1:38
verseText: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
jesusText: Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.

label: Mark 1:41
verseText: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
jesusText: I will; be thou clean.

label: Mark 1:44
verseText: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
jesusText: See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

label: Mark 2:14
verseText: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphæus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
jesusText: Follow me.

label: Mark 2:25–26
verseText: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
jesusText: Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?

label: Mark 3:3–5
verseText: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.

And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
jesusText: Stand forth.
Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill?
Stretch forth thine hand.

label: Mark 3:33–34
verseText: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
jesusText: Who is my mother, or my brethren?
Behold my mother and my brethren!

label: Mark 4:13
verseText: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
jesusText: Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?

label: Mark 4:35
verseText: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
jesusText: Let us pass over unto the other side.

label: Mark 4:39
verseText: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
jesusText: Peace, be still.

label: Mark 5:8–9
verseText: For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.
And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
jesusText: Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.
What is thy name?

label: Mark 5:19
verseText: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.
jesusText: Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.

label: Mark 5:30
verseText: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?
jesusText: Who touched my clothes?

label: Mark 5:39
verseText: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.
jesusText: Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.

label: Mark 5:41
verseText: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.
jesusText: Talitha cumi;

label: Mark 6:4
verseText: But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
jesusText: A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.

label: Mark 6:31
verseText: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
jesusText: Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while:

label: Mark 6:37–38
verseText: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
jesusText: Give ye them to eat.
How many loaves have ye? go and see.

label: Mark 6:50
verseText: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
jesusText: Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.

label: Mark 7:8
verseText: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
jesusText: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.

label: Mark 7:14
verseText: ¶ And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:
jesusText: Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:

label: Mark 7:16
verseText: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
jesusText: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

label: Mark 7:18–20
verseText: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.
jesusText: Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.

label: Mark 7:23
verseText: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.
jesusText: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.

label: Mark 7:27
verseText: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.
jesusText: Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.

label: Mark 7:29
verseText: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
jesusText: For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.

label: Mark 7:34
verseText: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
jesusText: Ephphatha,

label: Mark 8:2–3
verseText: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
jesusText: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.

label: Mark 8:5
verseText: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
jesusText: How many loaves have ye?

label: Mark 8:12
verseText: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
jesusText: Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.

label: Mark 8:15
verseText: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
jesusText: Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.

label: Mark 8:17–21
verseText: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
jesusText: Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up?
And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up?
How is it that ye do not understand?

label: Mark 8:26–27
verseText: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
jesusText: Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
Whom do men say that I am?

label: Mark 8:33
verseText: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
jesusText: Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

label: Mark 9:12–13
verseText: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.
jesusText: Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.

label: Mark 9:16
verseText: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?
jesusText: What question ye with them?

label: Mark 9:19
verseText: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
jesusText: O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.

label: Mark 9:21
verseText: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
jesusText: How long is it ago since this came unto him?

label: Mark 9:23
verseText: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
jesusText: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.

label: Mark 9:25
verseText: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
jesusText: Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.

label: Mark 9:29
verseText: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.
jesusText: This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.

label: Mark 9:33
verseText: ¶ And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?
jesusText: What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?

label: Mark 9:39–41
verseText: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
For he that is not against us is on our part.
For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.
jesusText: Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
For he that is not against us is on our part.
For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.

label: Mark 10:33–34
verseText: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
jesusText: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.

label: Mark 10:36
verseText: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
jesusText: What would ye that I should do for you?

label: Mark 10:51–52
verseText: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
jesusText: What wilt thou that I should do unto thee?
Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole.

label: Mark 11:2–3
verseText: And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.
And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.
jesusText: Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.
And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.

label: Mark 11:14
verseText: And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard it.
jesusText: No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever.

label: Mark 11:17
verseText: And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.
jesusText: Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.

label: Mark 11:26
verseText: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.
jesusText: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.

label: Mark 11:29–30
verseText: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.
jesusText: I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.

label: Mark 11:33
verseText: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
jesusText: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

label: Mark 12:34–37
verseText: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question.
¶ And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?
For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.
jesusText: Thou art not far from the kingdom of God.
How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?
For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.

label: Mark 13:2
verseText: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
jesusText: Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

label: Mark 14:6–9
verseText: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
jesusText: Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.

label: Mark 14:13–15
verseText: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.
jesusText: Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.

label: Mark 14:18
verseText: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
jesusText: Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.

label: Mark 14:20–21
verseText: And he answered and said unto them, It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
jesusText: It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.

label: Mark 14:27–28
verseText: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
jesusText: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.

label: Mark 14:30
verseText: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
jesusText: Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.

label: Mark 14:48–49
verseText: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?
I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
jesusText: Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?
I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.

label: Mark 14:72
verseText: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
jesusText: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.

label: Mark 15:2
verseText: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.
jesusText: Thou sayest it.
